positives: QD-OLED with unrivaled color accuracy and brightness
Deep black levels and vivid HDR highlights
Matte coating handling reflections in bright rooms
Wide viewing angles with consistent image quality
Four HDMI 2.1 ports supporting 4k @ 165Hz VRR
Near-instant pixel transitions for sharp motion
positives_detail: As a QD-OLED model, it displays colors that are rich, accurate, bright, and incredibly vivid.
Its panel produces inky blacks alongside bright highlights, making HDR content look incredible in dark rooms.
The impressive matte screen coating effectively eliminates reflections, even in the brightest spaces.
Image quality remains consistent at wide viewing angles, ideal for large rooms and off-axis seating.
Equipped with four HDMI 2.1 ports capable of 4k @ 165Hz with VRR, it pairs excellently with modern gaming consoles and PCs.
Pixel transitions are nearly instantaneous, ensuring fast motion appears crystal clear.
negatives: No Dolby Vision support
negatives_detail: Lacks Dolby Vision support, which may deter enthusiasts preferring that HDR format.

positives: QD-OLED panel with vivid colors and deep blacks
Bright HDR performance with punchy highlights
Wide viewing angles for consistent off-axis image
Four HDMI 2.1 ports supporting 4k @ 144Hz VRR
Fast pixel transitions for clear motion
positives_detail: Features a QD-OLED panel delivering vivid colors and inky blacks for excellent contrast.
Remains bright in HDR mode, making highlights pop in HDR content.
Maintains consistent image quality across wide viewing angles.
Includes four HDMI 2.1 ports capable of 4k @ 144Hz with VRR.
Offers nearly instantaneous pixel transitions for sharp motion clarity.
negatives: Glossy coating less effective at reducing reflections
No Dolby Vision support
negatives_detail: Uses a glossy screen coating, which is less effective at handling reflections in bright rooms.
Does not support Dolby Vision, offering only HDR10+.

positives: Effective local dimming with minimal haloing
High peak brightness suitable for bright rooms
Supports Dolby Vision and HDR10+ formats
Two HDMI 2.1 ports supporting 4k @ 144Hz and 1080p @ 288Hz VRR
Wide range of vibrant, punchy colors
positives_detail: Features effective local dimming that delivers bold blacks with minimal haloing around subtitles and highlights.
One of the brightest TVs on the market, offering solid reflection handling for bright room use.
Supports both Dolby Vision and HDR10+ for versatile HDR content compatibility.
Includes two HDMI 2.1 ports capable of 4k @ 144Hz and 1080p @ 288Hz with VRR.
Produces a wide range of bright and punchy colors suitable for any setting.
negatives: Limited viewing angles degrade at extreme angles
Only two HDMI 2.1 ports may limit multiple gaming setups
negatives_detail: Image quality degrades at extreme viewing angles, making it less ideal for very large rooms.
Only two HDMI 2.1 ports, which may limit connectivity for users with multiple gaming consoles.

negatives: No local dimming leads to grayish blacks

Slow pixel transitions causing motion blur

Slow pixel transitions lead to noticeable motion blur during fast action.
